mentions.note: Shedding slow-growth divisions; refocusing on fast-growing drugs; AAA balance sheet; emerging leadership stock in healthcare sector.

Core defensive holding in the charitable trust providing cushion during today's data-center meltdown. Strong new drug pipeline, triple-A balance sheet better than U.S. Preserved sanity during tsunami sell-off.
10 billion-dollar+ drugs, strong medtech pipeline, no major patent cliffs, 19x earnings multiple. Divesting commodity ortho biz raises multiple. Buy slowly.
AAA balance sheet healthcare leader suitable for long-term holdings including portfolios for young children and grandchildren.
Bessembinder research identified J&J as one of 17 stocks with 5 million percent cumulative returns; healthcare leader with secular growth.
Pharma play that didn't give up gains despite high-flyer data center selloff; healthcare sector solid today; recommended over volatile CoreWeave for risk-averse investors.
Alternative to Novo Nordisk; down from highs; great balance sheet; doing many things right; solid large-cap healthcare play.
One of two triple-A balance sheet names (other: MSFT); best pharmaceutical pipeline of blockbuster potential. Trades at <20x earnings—deep discount.
Buying into healthcare free fall; new psoriasis pill more convenient than injectable biologics; talc litigation resolved; orthopedics spinoff unlocks value; room to buy more on weakness.
Most blockbusters and best pipeline in pharma; transformed company shedding slower-growing divisions; expect morning selloff on release then recovery during call.
Tobacco settlement case example: stock fell from $140 to $100s on litigation narrative, then recovered $140→$230 when facts cleared; Meta will follow similar pattern but timing uncertain.

FDA approved Icotide (oral IL-23 pill) for plaque psoriasis; can eat AbbVie's $7.5B Skyrizi franchise; management $5B peak sales (Street $5.5–7.5B); spinoff/litigation fade add tailwinds. Stock wrongly priced down on macro.
Received green light on psoriasis pill (not injection), significant approval; stock down ~$1 despite positive news—stagflation sentiment crushing healthcare.
Terrific drug company and defensive blue chip; February winner in risk-off environment.
Healthcare and consumer products conglomerate making understandable products; benefits from rotation to comprehensible businesses with tangible output.
Solid earner with dividend and buyback; not expensive vs. rest of market; old-fashioned reliability in healthcare without Tony Soprano overtones.
Strong earnings with robust pharma growth (8% quarter, 5% FY despite Stellara LOE), MedTech cadence improving, Capilla $5B potential, oncology dominance.
Pure pharma play post-orthopedics spin; talc lawsuits less relevant; best pipeline in industry; report Wednesday.
Can thrive in weak economy; medicine always needed; rallying fast but deserves strength; good hedge alongside PG.
Down $5+ intraday; orthopedic spin-off removes slower-growth unit, raises core pharma valuation (prior KenView precedent); terrific entry even at -3.
